title: Fix PostHog E2E Tests Implementation Plan

tasks.title: Add test-mode branch to PostHog init (1/6)
tasks.task: Open `src/instrumentation-client.ts` and add a `declare global { interface Window { __E2E_MODE__?: boolean; } }` block after the existing imports

tasks.title: Add test-mode branch to PostHog init (2/6)
tasks.task: Add the line `const isE2E = typeof window !== "undefined" && window.__E2E_MODE__ === true;` after the global declaration

tasks.title: Add test-mode branch to PostHog init (3/6)
tasks.task: Inside the existing `posthog.init()` options object, add a conditional spread placeholder: `...(isE2E && { }),`

tasks.title: Add test-mode branch to PostHog init (4/6)
tasks.task: Inside the conditional spread object, add the key `request_batching: false`

tasks.title: Add test-mode branch to PostHog init (5/6)
tasks.task: Inside the same conditional spread object, add the key `disable_session_recording: true`

tasks.title: Add test-mode branch to PostHog init (6/6)
tasks.task: Add an inline comment directly above the conditional spread explaining: "Playwright E2E mode: disable batching so captures fire immediately and can be intercepted by AnalyticsHelper. Session recording is disabled to avoid test traffic leaking to the real PostHog project."

tasks.title: Inject flag in analytics fixture (1/4)
tasks.task: Open `e2e/fixtures/test.ts` and locate the `analytics` fixture body at lines 15-20

tasks.title: Inject flag in analytics fixture (2/4)
tasks.task: Before the `const analytics = new AnalyticsHelper(page)` line, add an `await page.addInitScript(() => { ... });` call

tasks.title: Inject flag in analytics fixture (3/4)
tasks.task: Inside the `addInitScript` callback, set `(window as Window & { __E2E_MODE__?: boolean }).__E2E_MODE__ = true`

tasks.title: Inject flag in analytics fixture (4/4)
tasks.task: Add a comment directly above the `addInitScript` call explaining it must run before any page script so the next `goto()` injects the flag into the fresh document where `instrumentation-client.ts` will read it

tasks.title: Phase 1 manual verification (1/2)

tasks.title: Phase 1 manual verification (2/2)

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (1/23)
tasks.task: Change the `toBeFired()` signature at `e2e/utils/analytics-helper.ts:233` from `toBeFired(): void` to `async toBeFired(timeoutMs = 5000): Promise<void>`

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (2/23)
tasks.task: Inside `toBeFired`, record `const startedAt = Date.now();` as the first line

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (3/23)
tasks.task: Wrap the existing match logic in a `while (Date.now() - startedAt < timeoutMs) { ... }` polling loop

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (4/23)
tasks.task: Inside the loop, filter `this.events` into `const matchingEvents = this.events.filter((e) => e.event === this.eventName);`

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (5/23)
tasks.task: Inside the loop, if `matchingEvents.length > 0 && this.propertyMatchers.length === 0`, `return` immediately (success path, no property matchers)

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (6/23)
tasks.task: Inside the loop, if `matchingEvents.length > 0` and there ARE property matchers, use `matchingEvents.find((event) => this.propertyMatchers.every(({ key, matcher }) => matcher(event.properties[key])))` and `return` when a fully-matching event is found

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (7/23)
tasks.task: At the end of each loop iteration, `await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 50));` to yield control

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (8/23)
tasks.task: After the loop (timeout branch), re-compute `matchingEvents` from the current `this.events` so the failure message reflects the final state

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (9/23)
tasks.task: Raise the existing `expect(matchingEvents.length, ...).toBeGreaterThan(0)` assertion with a message referencing `this.eventName`, `timeoutMs`, and the captured event names

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (10/23)
tasks.task: Build a `conditions` string by joining `this.propertyMatchers.map((m) => m.description)` with `", "`

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (11/23)
tasks.task: Build an `actualProps` string by joining `matchingEvents.map((e) => JSON.stringify(e.properties))` with newlines

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (12/23)
tasks.task: Throw a final `Error` with message `"Expected '${this.eventName}' with [${conditions}] within ${timeoutMs}ms, but no matching event found.\nActual '${this.eventName}' events:\n${actualProps}"`

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (13/23)
tasks.task: Change the `toBeFiredTimes()` signature at `e2e/utils/analytics-helper.ts:269` from `toBeFiredTimes(count: number): void` to `async toBeFiredTimes(count: number, timeoutMs = 5000): Promise<void>`

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (14/23)
tasks.task: Inside `toBeFiredTimes`, record `const startedAt = Date.now();` as the first line

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (15/23)
tasks.task: Wrap the existing match logic in a `while (Date.now() - startedAt < timeoutMs) { ... }` polling loop

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (16/23)
tasks.task: Inside the loop, compute `const matching = this.events.filter((e) => { if (e.event !== this.eventName) return false; if (this.propertyMatchers.length === 0) return true; return this.propertyMatchers.every(({ key, matcher }) => matcher(e.properties[key])); });`

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (17/23)
tasks.task: Inside the loop, if `matching.length === count`, `return` immediately (success)

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (18/23)
tasks.task: Inside the loop, if `matching.length > count`, `break` to fail fast on overshoot

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (19/23)
tasks.task: At the end of each loop iteration, `await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 50));`

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (20/23)
tasks.task: After the loop (timeout or overshoot branch), re-compute `matching` from the current `this.events`

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (21/23)
tasks.task: Raise `expect(matching).toHaveLength(count);` so Playwright surfaces its rich length-diff failure

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (22/23)
tasks.task: Verify `expectNoEvent` at `e2e/utils/analytics-helper.ts:161` remains sync — do NOT add `async` or `await` (per "What We're NOT Doing")

tasks.title: Promote toBeFired to async with polling (23/23)
tasks.task: Verify `waitForEvent` at `e2e/utils/analytics-helper.ts:138-153` is NOT touched — it already polls correctly and is used as-is by the Phase 1 smoke test

tasks.title: Phase 3 automated verification (1/4)
tasks.task: `make check` passes (typecheck — note: biome 2.4.9's `noFloatingPromises` rule is in nursery and NOT enabled in this project's `biome.json`, and TypeScript `strict` doesn't catch unawaited promises either. Static analysis will NOT catch a missed `await` on `.toBeFired()` — the checks below are the real safety net.)

tasks.title: Phase 3 automated verification (2/4)
tasks.task: Grep confirms zero `test.fixme` calls remain in the 6 analytics spec files: `rg "test\.fixme" e2e/features/cta-tracking.spec.ts e2e/features/booking-form.spec.ts e2e/features/login.spec.ts e2e/features/faq-interactions.spec.ts e2e/journeys/form-abandonment.spec.ts e2e/journeys/lead-conversion.spec.ts` returns nothing

tasks.title: Phase 3 automated verification (3/4)
tasks.task: Grep confirms every `.toBeFired(` or `.toBeFiredTimes(` call is preceded by `await`: `rg --multiline --multiline-dotall "^\s*analytics\b(?:(?!await).)*?\.toBeFired" e2e/features e2e/journeys` returns nothing

tasks.title: Phase 3 automated verification (4/4)
tasks.task: `make test_e2e` shows ≥ 200 passed (up from 145), ≤ 55 skipped (down from 111), 0 failed on the first run. If a test silently passed due to a missed `await` on an assertion, Playwright's unhandled-rejection handler surfaces it as a failure here — rerun after fixing.

tasks.title: Phase 3 manual verification (1/1)
tasks.task: Spot-check HTML report: click into 2-3 of the newly-passing tests and confirm the "events captured" count matches expectations (e.g., the `pricing tier CTAs` test should show both `pricing_foundation` and `pricing_growth` captures)

tasks.title: Run full suite and collect failures (1/5)
tasks.task: Confirm the working tree is clean (no unrelated diffs that could skew failures)

tasks.title: Run full suite and collect failures (2/5)
tasks.task: Run `make test_e2e` end-to-end against a clean worktree

tasks.title: Run full suite and collect failures (3/5)
tasks.task: Capture the JSON reporter output to a scratch file (e.g. `/tmp/posthog-triage.json`) for parsing

tasks.title: Run full suite and collect failures (4/5)
tasks.task: Parse the JSON into a list of `(test title, source file, error message)` tuples for the failures

tasks.title: Run full suite and collect failures (5/5)
tasks.task: Save the parsed failure list to a scratch file for use during triage

tasks.title: Triage failures (1/17)
tasks.task: For each failure in the parsed list, label it `property-mismatch`, `event-never-fires`, or `safeCapture-race`

tasks.title: Triage failures (2/17)
tasks.task: Note any failure that doesn't fit cleanly into one of those three buckets — surface it before continuing

tasks.title: Triage failures (3/17)
tasks.task: For each property-mismatch failure, open `src/lib/posthog.ts` and locate the `trackX()` call site that emits the event

tasks.title: Triage failures (4/17)
tasks.task: Compare the test's `withProperty(...)` keys/values against the app's actual capture payload and identify which side diverges

tasks.title: Triage failures (5/17)
tasks.task: Decide per failure whether to fix the test or the app — prefer fixing the test when the app shape is a deliberate schema, prefer fixing the app when it's a clear oversight

tasks.title: Triage failures (6/17)
tasks.task: Apply the chosen fix

tasks.title: Triage failures (7/17)
tasks.task: Re-run just that single test (e.g. `yarn test:e2e -g "<test title>"`) to confirm it passes

tasks.title: Triage failures (8/17)
tasks.task: For each "event never fires" failure, identify the user action in the test that should have triggered the event

tasks.title: Triage failures (9/17)
tasks.task: Trace that user action back to the corresponding handler in the app source

tasks.title: Triage failures (10/17)
tasks.task: Verify the handler calls the expected `trackX()` function from `src/lib/posthog.ts`

tasks.title: Triage failures (11/17)
tasks.task: If the `trackX()` call site is missing, add it

tasks.title: Triage failures (12/17)
tasks.task: Re-run that single test to confirm it passes

tasks.title: Triage failures (13/17)
tasks.task: For each suspected `safeCapture` race, look for `[PostHog] Not ready, event queued` in the test's page console output to confirm the diagnosis

tasks.title: Triage failures (14/17)
tasks.task: If confirmed, add a `waitForPostHogReady()` helper to `e2e/fixtures/test.ts` that calls `await page.waitForFunction(() => (window as any).posthog?.__loaded === true, { timeout: 5000 })`

tasks.title: Triage failures (15/17)
tasks.task: Export `waitForPostHogReady` (or expose it through the analytics fixture API) so tests can call it

tasks.title: Triage failures (16/17)
tasks.task: Call `waitForPostHogReady()` at the top of each test that hit the race

tasks.title: Triage failures (17/17)
tasks.task: Re-run each affected test to confirm it passes

tasks.title: Handle out-of-scope app gaps (1/5)
tasks.task: For each failure that requires non-trivial app-side instrumentation, draft a GitHub issue title and body describing the failing test, the missing event/property, and the proposed fix

tasks.title: Handle out-of-scope app gaps (2/5)
tasks.task: File the follow-up issues via `gh issue create`, capturing the returned issue numbers

tasks.title: Handle out-of-scope app gaps (3/5)
tasks.task: For each filed issue, re-apply `test.fixme("TODO: #<issue-number> — app-side instrumentation missing")` on the corresponding test (only — leave all other newly-unskipped tests untouched)

tasks.title: Handle out-of-scope app gaps (4/5)
tasks.task: After re-applying the targeted fixmes, re-run `make test_e2e` and confirm 0 failures (the deferred tests now skip, the rest pass)

tasks.title: Handle out-of-scope app gaps (5/5)
tasks.task: Run `make check` one more time to confirm typecheck and lint still pass with the targeted fixmes in place

tasks.title: Phase 4 automated verification (1/3)
tasks.task: `make test_e2e` reports 0 failed across all 3 viewport projects

tasks.title: Phase 4 automated verification (2/3)
tasks.task: Passed count is ≥ 200 (allowing for 2-3 Phase 4 follow-up fixmes if genuinely blocked on app work)

tasks.title: Phase 4 automated verification (3/3)
tasks.task: `make check` still passes

tasks.title: Phase 4 manual verification (1/3)
tasks.task: Visual inspection of the HTML report shows the formerly-skipped tests now in the "passed" column

tasks.title: Phase 4 manual verification (2/3)
tasks.task: Spot check one newly-passing test in each file to confirm the assertions are meaningful (not just passing because the capture array is empty and `expectNoEvent` is being used)

tasks.title: Phase 4 manual verification (3/3)
tasks.task: Open the PostHog project in the browser and confirm the test run did NOT create a flood of session recordings (validates that `disable_session_recording: true` kicked in)
